payload.text:
System Bet Calculator: https://betbuzz.net/betting-tools/system-bet-calculator
Arbitrage calculator: arbitrage calculator
Bet calculator: https://smarkets.com/getting-started/bet-calculator/
Odds converter: https://smarkets.com/getting-started/odds-converter/
matched betting calculator: https://smarkets.com/getting-started/matched-betting-calculator/

using the above links as examples, help me build an all-in-one app with different betting calculators. Make the app responsive

**Developing the App Features**

I'm now focusing on the core functionalities for this betting app. Specifically, I'm working to integrate a robust system bet calculator and an arbitrage calculator. These are the top priorities to ensure all user needs are met. I'm also ensuring all the app's components have responsive design with high-quality UI/UX. The plan is to get an MVP with these features done as quickly as possible.


**Refining the App's Structure**

I've decided to build this application using React with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS. The app's structure includes a dashboard housing various calculators: a bet calculator, arbitrage calculator, system bet calculator, odds converter, and a matched betting calculator. Furthermore, I am implementing integration with the Gemini API to provide expert betting insights and risk analysis. I am planning on using the API to explain the value of a bet. System bets present a challenge due to combinatorial math, so I'll need a helper function.


**Defining the Core Calculators**

I'm starting to formalize the mathematical formulas for odds conversion: fractional to decimal, decimal to American, and calculating probability from decimal odds. Also, the standard formula for matched betting is going into the calculations. Visually, I envision a clean, dark/light mode, financial look using Tailwind for grids and cards, and clear icons for each calculator.

entries.description: Application metadata and required permissions.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and Font Awesome for icons.
React entry point using React 18 createRoot API.
Global type definitions for betting calculators.
Core mathematical utility functions for betting calculations.
Gemini API service for providing betting insights and education.
Main application component with layout and routing logic.
Navigation sidebar for switching between different calculators.
Top header component with mobile toggle.
Standard bet calculator for single and accumulator bets.
Arbitrage calculator to identify risk-free betting opportunities.
Utility for converting between different odds formats.
Calculator for matched betting strategies (Free bets and Qualifying bets).
System bet calculator (Trixie, Yankee, Patent, etc.) for multiple selections.
Welcome dashboard with quick navigation cards.

payload.text: have a setting to set a global odds type as some users might prefer to use american or fractional over decimal. Place the setting in the app settings

**Considering Global Odds Type**

I'm currently focused on incorporating a global "Odds Type" setting (American, Fractional, Decimal) that applies across the calculators. The "App Settings" (likely accessible via the gear icon) seems the best place for it.


**Implementing Odds Format Settings**

I've made progress on the odds format settings. I've decided to use a context or global state to manage the chosen `OddsFormat`. I have also completed the basic conversion functions; specifically, `decimalToAmerican`, and `decimalToFractional`, the plan is to use decimal internally for calculations to keep things simpler. Now I need to implement the settings modal to allow users to select their preferred odds format, and then update the calculators to use the global setting.


**Refining Calculation Logic**

I'm now refining the calculation logic. I've decided to create `toDecimal` and `fromDecimal` functions within `utils/calculations.ts`, taking an odds value and format to a decimal, and back to a string format, respectively. This should streamline things significantly. I'm also planning a reusable `OddsInput` component now, to handle the odds input fields across different calculators, which would hopefully keep things simple.

entries.description: Enhance conversion utilities to support bi-directional odds conversion for all formats.
Add global settings state and Settings Modal logic.
Add settings click handler to the gear icon.
New component for managing application-wide settings like Odds Format.
Updated to respect global odds format for selections.
Support global odds format for arbitrage calculations.

payload.text: for the system calculator, implement something similar to the image.png

**Analyzing Visual Style**

I'm currently focused on visually analyzing the provided image to understand its style. I've noted a distinct input section with dual dropdown menus, seemingly representing a 'k out of n' system, specifically "2 out of 3" in the example. I'm taking notes.


**Refining K-Out-Of-N Logic**

I've been refining the k-out-of-n logic, specifically noting the need to update `SystemCalculator.tsx`. I'll also check `utils/calculations.ts` to see if `getCombinations` requires adjustment. My focus is on the more dynamic k-out-of-n system selection, which is the key feature. I'll utilize SVG for the bar chart implementation. I'm focusing on the implementation details now.


**Clarifying System Mechanics**

I'm now clarifying the system mechanics further. I've realized `k` represents the chosen system size, and the image confirms this with examples like "System 2" and "System 4" visualized. I'm noting that bets are placed on all combinations of size `k` from `n` selections, which informs the stake per row calculation. I'm also confirming the image data, such as "Bets," "Rows," and "Return" values, to ensure all underlying mechanics are correct. I need to make sure void handling is correct. I'm focusing on the comparisons shown in the chart.

entries.description: Redesigned System Calculator to match the provided image, including dynamic k/n system selection, individual bet outcomes, result summary, and a visual comparison chart.
